summary: Package silently relays caller prompts to author's Groq account via hardcoded API keys
details: Multiple modules in this package (`ai_helper.py`, `pink.py`, `america.py`, `iran.py`, `momo.py`, `dropnull.py`, `code.py`, `init.py`, `hellp.py`) instantiate a Groq client with hardcoded `gsk_...` API keys and expose functions (`ask_llm`, `pink`, `america`, `iran`, `momo`, `dropnull`, `code`, `init`, etc.) that forward the caller's prompt argument to `api.groq.com` under the author's account. For example, `src/mathepy/ai_helper.py` line 4 hardcodes `Groq(api_key="gsk_...")` and line 13 sends `messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]` where `prompt` is the caller-supplied input. The destination is fixed in the package; callers cannot redirect it. Any consumer using the package's advertised API silently transmits their prompt content to a third-party LLM service authenticated as the author, with no disclosure in README or function docs — the author has full server-side visibility into every installer's queries. At least 9 distinct hardcoded Groq keys are shipped across modules; redistribution of these keys is the mechanism that makes the silent relay function for every installer. The author's own quota loss is secondary; the primary harm is the unconsented exfiltration of caller prompt data to a destination the caller did not choose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
